item.title: 11:49 am, February 28, 2026
item.description: A little bit more axe wrangling, trying to get the big blocks in place on the upper and lower blades. Definitely needs a good old scuffing and weathering pass.

item.title: 6:45 pm, February 27, 2026
item.description: Immediately sidetracked by non-metallic metal. The eventual headstone of every mini-painter.

item.title: 5:53 pm, February 26, 2026
item.description: Finally starting into a slow expansion of the tattoo. This is going to take a billion years.

item.title: 8:28 pm, February 22, 2026
item.description: Starting to sketch out what a full-sleeve tattoo might look like for a fantasy dorf. Ever get the feeling you’ve bitten off absurdly more than you can chew?

item.title: 6:23 pm, February 22, 2026
item.description:
Why yes, I did just add a massively passive-agressive self-shaming /now page to my blog, thank you for asking.

https://fernseed.org/now/

item.title: 4:46 pm, February 21, 2026
item.description: Beefy boi.

item.title: 1:19 pm, February 21, 2026
item.description:
Starting on the shadow-side arm fleshtones.

Aside: I will always attempt skin ‘from black’ in the future. If you build up in thin layers you get such a developing sense of translucency for free.

item.title: 10:39 am, February 21, 2026
item.description: A further ‘drama’ pass on the hair.

item.title: 7:01 pm, February 20, 2026
item.description: Hairdressing intensifies

item.title: 5:23 pm, February 20, 2026
item.description: Look at this absolute shambles of a workspace. Very much reflecting the mind behind it.

item.title: 8:45 am, February 20, 2026
item.description: The thing currently being sold as artificial intelligence reads a lot like the Chinese Room problem, if written by Borges.

item.title: 7:12 pm, February 19, 2026
item.description: First beard(s) highlight pass sketched in.

item.title: 6:00 pm, February 19, 2026
item.description: Right. Let’s get started on that hair then. All of which is entirely composed of additional beards.

item.title: 7:50 pm, February 18, 2026
item.description: In contrast, the first ‘real’ (fake) highlight placement.

item.title: 6:32 pm, February 18, 2026
item.description: Interestingly, at this kind of size, at this distance from my painting lamp, the real light reflection reads much more scale-appropriate, so it looks like I’ve been highlighting here when I really haven’t.

item.title: 6:18 pm, February 18, 2026
item.description: Tiny eye dots, tiny teef dots, and a patchy first couple of base layers for the skin, over black. The fool’s not even using a zenithal.

item.title: 6:36 pm, February 17, 2026
item.description:
Okay. Now hear me out. Have I abandoned yet another 80%* complete mini because every part of my flesh and skin and soul rebels at the thought of ever even looking at it again? Yes. Technically, yes.

But! I’ve now picked up a vastly larger, more fine-detailed display figure that will need exponentially more patience and skill and time to complete. I see this as a clear win.

*~40-80%

item.title: 11:32 am, February 16, 2026
item.description: Just realised that my own ‘my roman empire’ may be the 2007 Dairy Milk gorilla ad.

item.title: 8:32 pm, February 13, 2026
item.description: Just look at that boopable snoot.

item.title: 9:03 pm, February 12, 2026
item.description:
Yay!

We’re definitely into the golden age of the series now.
